calendar.matters: PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JACK E. BROWN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-11-2026 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Quinio, Esq. 916-288-1394 Pacific Legal Foundation for Plaintiff.
John George, Esq. Edmund Wang, Esq. 415-554-4783 Office of the City Attorney for Defendants.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED.

Mr. Quinio contests the tentative ruling and presents arguments; contests sustaining the demurrer and reasons for the ruling set forth in paragraphs 5-8; ordinance, implement reparation plan, tax payer's challenge, cites Connerly case, race neutral policy as a poison pill, unlawful expenditure.
Court comments, poses questions; taxpayer standing, rightness, nature of calims and elements.
Court and Counsel in discussion.
Mr. George responds and present brief points on the plan - high level aspirational document and ordinance; cites section A and C, can be administered in a race neutral way.

After intelligent arguments, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Respondents and Defendants San Francisco Human Rights Commission, Mawuli Tugbenyoh and the City and County of San Francsico's demurrer to the complaint.

Taken Under Submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Apr-08-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' unopposed Motion to Admit Pro Hac Vice Counsel Jack E. Brown is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, counsel Jack E. Brown may appear as counsel pro hac vice for Moving Parties in this action.

Moving Parties shall prepare a proposed order which repeats the above text verbatim and email.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
